history.content:
<thought>
I can see a wall (#) directly ahead in the column with the doorway. The cell in front of me is unknown (?), but moving into lava (L) is dangerous. Turning left or right could reveal an opening around the wall; forward seems blocked by the wall line. I will turn right to look for a safe path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

CURRENT OBSERVATION:
L.L.#??
L.L.#??
....#??
L.L.#??
L.L.#??
L.L.#??
L.L^#??

<thought>
Ahead of me is a wall (#), so I cannot move forward. To search for a safe route toward the goal, I will turn left to change direction and look for open floor (.) or the goal (G) rather than lava (L).
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
My view is the same as before: there is a wall (#) directly in front, so moving forward is not possible. I previously turned right; to keep searching for an opening, I will turn left instead.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I can see a wall (#) directly in front, so MOVE_FORWARD is not possible. There are lava (L) cells to my right/front area, so I should turn left to look for a safe floor cell or the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I still have a wall directly in front (a #). To find a passage, I’ll turn right and scan for a safe (.) or the goal (G).
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
Now that I turned right, the cell in front is still a wall (#), so moving forward is blocked. I need to change direction again to avoid the wall and find a safe path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I can see a wall column at my left, but the cell in front of me is a floor '.' (not lava). Moving forward seems safe and may lead toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
